title: AI Agent Onboarding
riido_task: RIID-4720 [Contracts] AI Agent onboarding fixture projection
summary: AI Agent onboarding fixture behavior is generated from the client API DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture set; policy prose is not a second authority.
invariants: client-local onboarding draft/configuration selection remains non-durable until workspace_id and runtime_id are known.
fixture-created agents are ordinary agents with non-unique names; the control plane must not auto-suffix display names.
Direct setting is client presentation, not an AgentOnboardingFixture.
Runtime/provider install rows are presentation over device/runtime liveness, not provider-install commands.

loop.observation: Onboarding fixture rules existed in the AI Agent policy prose and API fixture scenarios, but no focused CI artifact proved the fixture catalog, direct-setting boundary, and final create routes together.
loop.hypothesis: A small verifier can make onboarding drift observable by deriving a reader and evidence from DSL, IR, OpenAPI, and product fixture-row anchors.
loop.execute: Verify onboarding list/create routes, direct create routes, fixture schema fields, create request fields, scenario count, OpenAPI projections, and no-diff provider/waitlist boundaries.
loop.evaluate: CI fails when onboarding operation tuples, schema fields, fixture row anchors, generated reader text, or no-diff route boundaries drift.
loop.retrospective: Future onboarding behavior should update this manifest and API fixture first, then regenerate the reader instead of editing long-form policy prose.
